content.problemStatement:
In a cricket match, there are two teams, each comprising 111111 players. The scorecard of the match lists the runs scored and wickets taken by each of these 222222 players.

To determine the "Man of the Match", we assess each player's performance. Points are awarded to a player as follows:


Each run scored earns 111 point.
Every wicket taken earns 202020 points.
The player with the **highest total points** is awarded the "Man of the Match" title.

You are given the scorecard of a cricket match, listing the contributions of all 222222 players.

The players are numbered from 111 to 222222. Find the "Man of the Match".

It is guaranteed that for all inputs to this problem, the "Man of the Match" is **unique**.

**Note**: A player who belongs to the losing team can also win the "Man of the Match" award.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of 222222 lines of input. The iii-th of these 222222 lines contains two space-separated integers AiA_iAi​ and BiB_iBi​ — respectively, the runs scored and wickets taken by the iii-th player.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a single integer iii (1≤i≤22)(1 \leq i \leq 22)(1≤i≤22) denoting the index of the player with the maximum score.


The tests for this problem are designed such that there will be exactly one player with the maximum score.
constraints: All calculations fit in 64‑bit signed integer
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, exactly 222222 lines follow. The i‑th of these lines contains two space‑separated integers A_i and B_i — the runs scored and wickets taken by player i (players are indexed from 1).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the index (1‑based) of the player with the maximum total points. It is guaranteed that this index is unique.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Player 1: 10*111 = 1110 points. Player 2: 1*202020 = 202020 points. Player 3: 5*111 + 2*202020 = 555 + 404040 = 404595 points, which is the highest, so the answer is index 3.

solution.approach: For each player compute points = runs*111 + wickets*202020. While scanning keep the best score and its index. After processing all 222222 players of a test case output the stored index. The algorithm is a single linear pass per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * 222222) – each player is processed once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are stored.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    PLAYER_COUNT = 222222
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        best_score = -1
        best_idx = -1
        for i in range(1, PLAYER_COUNT + 1):
            runs = int(next(it))
            wickets = int(next(it))
            score = runs * 111 + wickets * 202020
            if score > best_score:
                best_score = score
                best_idx = i
        out_lines.append(str(best_idx))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
